titleStmt.title: Letter from Francis Sheehy Skeffington to Mrs McGarry, 10 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Francis Sheehy Skeffington

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

msIdentifier.collection: Sheehy Skeffington Papers, Collection List No. 47; Ms 33,612/18-35

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Private 11, Grosvenor Place,
Rathmines, Dublin.
10 — I — 16. Dear Mrs. McGarry, In September, 1914, to meet pressing demands of the printer for
a reduction of the debt on the IRISH CITIZEN, I raised a bank loan
of £100, guaranteed by three good signatures in addition to my own.
I have not been able, owing to the continued strain on the IRISH
CITIZEN, to reduce the principal of this loan since; and now one of
the guarantors wishes to withdraw her name, because she is in favour
of conscription and objects to our opposition to it. The other two
guarantors are both perfectly good securities for the amount, but in
these days of extra financial stringency the bank will require a
third name to replace the one that is withdrawn. Could you poss-
ibly help in this difficulty, or suggest to me the name of someone
who could? With many thanks for your kind sympathy during my recent exper-
iences, and best wishes for the new year, Francis Sheehy Skeffington
